metadata.description:
Psychoacoustic bass enhancement for Reaper.
PsychoBass adds upper harmonics to bass signals so they remain audible on small speakers and earbuds, using timbre-matched harmonic generation that preserves the character of the original instrument.
See https://github.com/stevewcox/psychobass for full documentation.

Psychoacoustic bass enhancement using timbre-matched harmonic generation.
Adds upper harmonics to bass signals so they remain audible on small speakers and earbuds, without the synthetic character of naive harmonic generators.
Based on published Virtual Bass System research (Ben-Tzur/Colloms AES 1999, Larsen & Aarts JAES 2002, Mu/Gan/Tan ICASSP 2013).
Controls: Frequency - Crossover point (40-200 Hz). Set just above your lowest note. Intensity - Harmonic generation amount (0-100%) Original Bass - Level of sub-bass in output (0-100%) Harmonics Mix - Blend of generated harmonics (0-100%) Output Gain - Output trim (-24 to +10 dB) Harmonic Tilt - Roll-off of upper harmonics (0-6 dB/oct) Harmonic Style - Warm (2nd+3rd), Tight (odd), Full (broadest)
Suggested starting point for bass guitar: Frequency: 85Hz, Intensity: 45%, Original Bass: 75%, Harmonics Mix: 55%, Tilt: 4dB, Style: Warm

version.author: PsychoBass Contributors
version.changelog:
Initial release.
- Feedback-multiplier NLD with exponential soft-clip - 4th-order Butterworth crossover - Timbre-matched envelope compander - Harmonic high-pass at 1.5x crossover (eliminates comb-filter notches) - Spectral tilt high-shelf cut (0-6 dB/oct) - Three harmonic styles: Warm, Tight, Full - Stereo processing - Output gain: -24dB to +10dB
